title: Heat, technology, and napping impact health according to multiple scientific studies
summary: Various scientific studies warn about the effects of heat on blood and decision-making, technological fatigue in nursing, the role of napping in memory, and the application of AI to generate peptides.

title: Key advances in artificial intelligence: autonomous agents, interpretability, and debate on employment
summary: AI models reduce costs and improve speed, agents browse websites and play Slay the Spire, while OpenAI discusses job creation and S&P warns of Oracle risk.

title: New hardware: AMD processors, MiniLED monitors, power supplies, and PC cases
summary: AMD launches themed CPUs and tests FSR 8X, HKC presents a 31.4-inch MiniLED monitor, MSI Afterburner improves maps, Thermaltake launches case for two PCs, and Cooler Master revises 750W power supply.

title: The technology market in Spain: material shortages, rising import costs, and new products
summary: The shortage of copper and fiber, the increase in shipping costs from China, guides to unify game launchers and choose controllers or SSDs, and the success of the Windrose device mark the current news.

title: Data centers: record consumption in Ireland, political controversy, and pollution in Wyoming
summary: Irish data centers consumed 23% of the national electricity, construction of a Meta center in Wyoming generates pollution and rare bacteria, and the issue enters election campaigns.

title: The rise of AI-generated content degrades quality on LinkedIn and other platforms
summary: Journalists are replaced by synthetic articles, LinkedIn is flooded with AI posts and fake job offers, and experts warn of a knowledge reset in online communities.

title: Lenovo presents gaming and professional laptops with Ryzen AI processors and RTX 5070 graphics
summary: The new Legion and YOGA incorporate AMD Ryzen AI Max+ CPUs, RTX 5070 and Radeon 8060S GPUs, with prices above $3,375.

title: US universities ban electronic devices to combat the use of AI
summary: The University of Chicago bans computers and phones in law classes, while a Brown professor notes that grades drop 48% without AI help on exams.

title: Meta appeals verdict on social media addiction and removes AI image generation feature
summary: Meta appeals a ruling blaming it for social media addiction, the winning lawyer calls it a 'holy war', and the company disables Muse which generated photos without consent on Instagram.

title: Windows 11 Copilot consumes memory as administrators criticize Microsoft and anecdotes of Windows 95 are recalled
summary: The Copilot assistant uses 1 GB of RAM, IT administrators are fed up with Microsoft's applications, and a veteran recalls how Windows 95 guessed installers.

title: The first social network is 57,000 years old and users abandon Netflix series and status updates
summary: A study reveals that hunter-gatherers already formed social networks, Netflix subscribers leave series after the first episode, and most Americans have stopped posting on social media.

title: Apple sues OpenAI for theft of trade secrets and auctions Apple-1 computer
summary: Apple files a lawsuit against OpenAI for stealing secrets, while Sotheby's auctions a functioning Apple-1 from the first series.

title: Linux 7.2-rc3 includes fixes for the Dreamcast console and small emulators gain popularity
summary: The Linux kernel continues to support the legendary Dreamcast console, while enthusiasts highlight the development of Tiny Emulators to preserve classic video games.

title: Robotaxis under scrutiny and proposals to reduce traffic congestion
summary: TechCrunch reports on an ultimatum in the robotaxi sector, while Hacker News discusses strategies to alleviate urban traffic jams.

title: First menstrual study in space and approval of orbital mirrors to illuminate Earth
summary: Two female scientists will travel in 2027 to research menstruation in microgravity, and the FCC authorizes satellites with giant mirrors to reflect sunlight.

title: New sodium battery charges in 4 minutes and electric car sales in Europe show an uneven map
summary: A sodium battery offers ultra-fast charging and long life, while electric vehicle sales in Europe reflect large differences between countries, led by the Scandinavian peninsula.

title: Laws against price surveillance in supermarkets and platforms to share subscriptions in Spain
summary: Three US states ban price surveillance in supermarkets, while in Spain legal platforms emerge to share costs of digital subscriptions.

title: Big tech companies are accused of stealing data and using AI as an excuse for their decisions
summary: One article denounces that tech companies steal data and undermine democracy, while another points out that companies use AI as a pretext to cut back or expand.

title: Hobbyists build their own GPU and design their first printed circuit at home
summary: The maker community demonstrates that it is possible to manufacture a GPU from home, while other enthusiasts share experiences designing and assembling PCB boards.

title: A Brazilian YouTuber recreates Photoshop tools in GIMP for free and open source
summary: The content creator shows how GIMP can imitate the main functions of Photoshop, offering a free alternative to Adobe software.
